subject: Astronomy & Space
title: What Are Cosmic Rays?
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on What Are Cosmic Rays?.

hook.text: Cosmic rays are high-energy particles from space that constantly bombard Earth. Most are protons moving near light speed.

buildup.text: They come from the Sun, exploding stars, and mysterious sources beyond our galaxy. Their energies span a huge range.

discovery.text: Victor Hess discovered them in 1912 by carrying instruments in a balloon. Radiation increased with altitude—it came from space.

twist.text: Earth's atmosphere and magnetic field shield us. On Mars or in deep space, cosmic rays pose a serious risk to astronauts.

climax.text: The highest-energy cosmic rays carry as much energy as a baseball pitch—packed into a single subatomic particle.

punchline.text: Particles from exploding stars hitting us constantly. Our atmosphere keeps us safe.

quiz.question: How were cosmic rays first discovered?
options: Victor Hess found that radiation increased with altitude during balloon flights in 1912
They were detected by the first radio telescopes in the 1930s
Astronauts on the International Space Station measured them directly
